story.soThat: services can declare and receive their dependencies automatically

tasks.task: Design DI container interface and core architecture
Add lifecycle management and dependency scopes

acceptanceCriteria.criteria: Container Interface and Implementation - sdk/di package provides Container interface and implementation with registration and resolution capabilities
Interface-based Registration - Container supports registering implementations against interface types with type safety
Lifecycle Management - Container resolves dependencies with proper lifecycle management (singleton, transient) and supports dependency scopes
Circular Dependency Detection - Container prevents infinite loops by detecting and reporting circular dependencies during registration or resolution
Error Handling - Container provides clear error messages for missing or misconfigured dependencies with actionable guidance
Factory Pattern Support - Container supports both constructor injection and factory patterns for flexible dependency creation

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: All service constructors accept dependencies as interface parameters. Framework eliminates hard-coded singleton dependencies. Framework supports both constructor injection and factory patterns.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Lightweight Custom DI Container with interface-based registration, type-safe resolution using Go generics, lifecycle management (Singleton, Transient, Scoped), zero runtime reflection overhead.

artifact.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Type-safe dependency injection container with interface registration and resolution, supporting Register[T](), Resolve[T](), Validate() methods and Singleton/Transient scopes.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: All services use constructor injection for dependencies. Goal: Developers can compose services cleanly and customize implementations.

artifact.reason: Base interface pattern for repository dependency injection
Existing service interface for dependency injection
Framework initializer that needs DI container integration
Repository with hard-coded MongoDB client (GetMongoClient) that needs injection
Core service struct that needs constructor injection
Hybrid service struct that needs constructor injection
Shows current service instantiation pattern that will use DI

package.name: go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver
package.purpose: MongoDB database driver for repository implementations

package.name: github.com/stretchr/testify
package.purpose: Testing framework with mocking and assertions

constraints.constraint: Implements Decision 1: Lightweight Custom DI Container from architecture document
Zero runtime reflection overhead, interface references are pointer-sized
Use Go generics for compile-time type checking of registrations
Explicit registration and resolution, no automatic dependency discovery
Support easy mocking through interface-based registration
Foundation for Epic 2's dependency injection patterns in EndorService and EndorHybridService
Follow Go philosophy - no magic reflection, keep DI simple and explicit
Structured errors with dependency context and resolution path

interface.name: Container
interface.signature: Register[T any](impl T, scope Scope) error; RegisterFactory[T any](factory func() (T, error), scope Scope) error; Resolve[T any]() (T, error); Validate() error

interface.name: RepositoryPattern
interface.signature: Base contract for CRUD repository operations with domain error handling

interface.name: EndorServiceInterface
interface.signature: GetResource() string; GetDescription() string; GetMethods() map[string]EndorServiceAction

tests.standards: Framework uses testify/mock for behavior verification, interface compliance tests using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern, integration tests with testutils.NewInMemoryRepository pattern, and build tags for unit vs integration test separation.
tests.locations: test/ (integration tests), sdk/*_test.go (unit tests), sdk/testutils/ (testing utilities and mocks)

ideas.test: Unit test container registration and resolution with mock implementations
Test type-safe registration of interfaces with concrete implementations
Test singleton vs transient scopes, verify dependency instance management
Test container rejects circular dependencies with clear error messages
Test missing dependency resolution returns structured errors with context
Test factory function registration and resolution with dependency context
Test container integration with EndorInitializer and service construction
